title: Japan’s biggest card network taps Circle to bring stablecoins to 40 million merchants
summary: Japan’s largest card network, JCB, has signed a memorandum of understanding with Circle to explore using USDC for cross-border payments and merchant transactions. The partnership could bring stablecoin payments to 40 million merchants as Japan ramps up efforts to integrate stablecoins into everyday commerce.
body:
Japan’s largest card network, JCB, has signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Circle, the issuer of the USDC stablecoin, to explore using USDC for cross-border payments and merchant transactions. The collaboration is part of Japan’s broader effort to integrate stablecoins into everyday commerce, potentially impacting 40 million merchants nationwide.

Under the MoU, JCB and Circle will work together to develop solutions that enable USDC transactions. This includes facilitating smoother cross-border payments and enhancing the efficiency of merchant transactions. The partnership highlights the growing acceptance of stablecoins in traditional financial systems.

For everyday people, this means that stablecoin payments could become more accessible and mainstream in Japan. Merchants may soon be able to accept USDC, reducing reliance on traditional banking systems and potentially lowering transaction fees. Consumers could also benefit from faster and cheaper cross-border payments.

The collaboration comes as Japan ramps up its efforts to position itself as a leader in the stablecoin space. The country has been actively working on regulatory frameworks to support the use of stablecoins in commerce. This partnership with JCB and Circle is a significant step toward achieving that goal.

For those interested in stablecoin adoption, this development is worth watching. The timeline for implementation is not yet clear, but the partnership signals a growing trend toward integrating stablecoins into traditional financial networks. Merchants and consumers in Japan should stay tuned for updates on when USDC payments become available.
